compiled_state.title: Sessions never dispatch multiple exploration agents in parallel to answer questions
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Problem

When a session needs to explore the codebase to answer a question, only a single exploration agent is dispatched. Multiple exploration agents should be used in parallel when the question spans different parts of the codebase or requires exploring multiple angles simultaneously.

## Expected Behavior

Complex exploration questions (e.g., cross-cutting codebase questions, multi-system investigations) should dispatch multiple exploration sub-agents in parallel, similar to how sprint dispatches multiple implementation sub-agents.

## Actual Behavior

Only one exploration agent is ever used, making exploration tasks slower and less thorough than they could be.

## Fix Direction

Investigate where exploration agents are dispatched (likely in debug-everything or similar skills) and add multi-agent parallel dispatch for complex exploration tasks.

comments.body: Previous epic 2e8e-72d9 (Incremental Exploration: Split large codebase and web search tasks into bounded sub-tasks, now closed) was intended to break large exploration tasks into smaller sub-tasks via an exploration-decomposition.md protocol. That epic established SINGLE_SOURCE vs MULTI_SOURCE classification and bounded decomposition (1 level), but dispatched to a single exploration agent per sub-question. This bug tracks the gap: even with decomposition, sub-questions are handled sequentially by one agent at a time rather than dispatching multiple exploration agents in parallel. The fix should leverage the existing exploration-decomposition.md protocol but add parallel dispatch of sub-question agents when MULTI_SOURCE classification fires.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral — ticket references agent guidance/skill behavior gap. Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fix applied: added Caller Dispatch Protocol section to exploration-decomposition.md specifying parallel Agent tool dispatch when MULTI_SOURCE fires. Root cause: Multi-Step Reasoning Drift + Positional Bias — DECOMPOSE_RECOMMENDED defined classification and signal format but omitted dispatch protocol (parallel vs sequential).
comments.author: Test
